title: Narrative Arc — The Corpus Is Manufactured, Not Found
recognition: Valuable knowledge is fragmented across conversations, files, frameworks, and operating experience.
reframe: A useful corpus is not simply discovered; it is deliberately manufactured from those fragments.
framework: Source, proof, canonical artifact, narrative arc, visual package, publication, and feedback form one manufacturing system.
proof: The repository already contains governed records, ledgers, publication packages, visual briefs, and a portable runtime skeleton.
application: New source material enters through the same gates and becomes reusable written, visual, and operational assets.
consequence: Each completed artifact strengthens the corpus and reduces the cost of producing future high-quality work.
invitation: Evaluate knowledge work as a compounding system rather than a sequence of isolated posts.
